gradeLabel: Open weights under a permissive license

ladderVerdict.justification: The MIT license permits commercial use, modification, and redistribution, requiring only retention of the copyright and license notice; this satisfies the criteria for L2 (open weights under a permissive license).

license.name: MIT
notAllowed: Remove the MIT license or copyright notice
license.commercialNote: MIT permits commercial use, modification and redistribution provided the copyright and license notice are retained.

technical.quantizationImpact: Moving from BF16 (≈1.34 TB VRAM for the full 671B‑parameter dense equivalent) to GGUF Q4_K_M reduces VRAM to ~400 GB, a ~70% reduction that enables multi‑GPU deployment.

private.text: The full DeepSeek-V3 model requires ~400 GB VRAM in Q4_K_M quantization, which exceeds the capacity of a single consumer GPU, making local deployment impractical for most individuals without access to multi‑GPU servers or cloud instances.

enterprise.text: Enterprises can meet the hardware requirements with multi‑GPU servers or cloud instances; the MIT license permits commercial use, modification, and redistribution with only attribution required.

nonprofit.text: Nonprofits with access to sufficient GPU resources (e.g., via research grants or cloud credits) can deploy the model under the permissive MIT license; otherwise, smaller community‑distilled versions may be more feasible.

disagreements: Some sources (e.g., source 4, 7, 9) discuss DeepSeek V3.2 or V3.5 specifications (VRAM ~1.4 TB) which pertain to a different version and should not be conflated with DeepSeek V3.
